# scroll_100k QA artifacts

Outputs of [`scripts/qa_viz.py`](../../../../scripts/qa_viz.py) over the
`scroll_100k` headline run. Refreshed by hand after each cost-sweep
re-run; not auto-generated by `make scroll-100k`.

The script's job is narrow: catch the things a single eyeballing of
`report.json` misses â€” cost-field arithmetic that doesn't add up,
degenerate "win" cells where a policy prunes essentially everything,
schema drift, missing or duplicated cells in the sweep grid,
extractor-source mixing, threshold-policy â„“-monotonicity violations,
and apples-to-oranges comparisons across different RAM budgets.

## How to regenerate

```bash
# point at the sweep dir (recommended â€” captures every (policy, â„“) cell)
make qa-viz REPORT=testdata/runs/scroll_100k/sweep_v2

# or at a single bundled report
make qa-viz REPORT=testdata/runs/scroll_100k/report.json
```

Both write into `testdata/runs/scroll_100k/qa/` by default.
Override with `QA_OUT=<dir>` (e.g. `qa_v3` for a parallel sweep).
Add `--strict` (`make qa-viz REPORT=... -- --strict` is awkward, so
invoke the script directly when you need it) to fail the run on any
schema / cost-arithmetic / source-mixing / monotonic-trend issue.

## Repro checklist

When citing numbers from these QA artifacts, record:

- Chain + observation window `[start,end)` and the command used.
- `data_source` stamp (do not compare across sources).
- The sweep grid (policies, â„“ values) and cost params.
- Repo commit SHA and any ingestion limits/skips (if present).

Output is deterministic â€” two runs against the same input produce
byte-identical files except for the `out_dir` field in
`qa_summary.json` when `QA_OUT` differs.

## Files

| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `qa_summary.json` | Top-level summary: cell count, policies, â„“ values, plus a rolled-up view of every check below. |
| `schema_issues.json` | Cells with missing/wrong-type/out-of-range fields. Catches drift earlier than cost arithmetic does. |
| `grid_gaps.json` | Missing or duplicated `(policy, â„“)` cells against the cartesian product of observed axes. |
| `degeneracy_flags.json` | Cells where all three degeneracy thresholds tripped, with reasons. |
| `monotonic_violations.json` | `statistical` / `statistical-robust` cells that violate the expected â„“-monotonicity. |
| `same_ram_matches.json` | Auto-paired `(policy_a, policy_b, â„“)` triples where `\|Î”RAMRatio\| < eps`. |
| `cost_decomposition.svg` | Stacked `RAMCost + MissPenaltyAgg = TotalCost` bars per cell. |
| `pareto_ramratio_coverage.svg` | Scatter of `RAMRatio` (x) vs `HotHitCoverage` (y), one dot per cell. |
| `qa_report.html` | Self-contained one-page dashboard embedding the two SVGs and every check table. |

## QA checks (in order of execution)

### 1. Schema sanity

For every cell verify each required field exists, is the right type, is
finite, and falls in the right range:

| Field group | Constraint |
|---|---|
| `Policy` | non-empty string |
| `RAMRatio`, `HotHitCoverage`, `FalsePruneRate`, `StorageSavedFrac` | number âˆˆ [0, 1] |
| `RAMCost`, `MissPenaltyAgg`, `TotalCost`, `TotalSlots`, `TotalExposure`, `SlotBlocksPruned`, `SlotBlocksHot`, `ObservedIntervals`, `CensoredIntervals`, `Reactivations`, `FinalPrunedSlots` | finite number â‰¥ 0 |

Drift here usually means a units/scale bug (e.g. percent vs fraction)
or a renamed field. Caught earlier than the cost-arithmetic check
because it fails closed even when `TotalCost` happens to add up after
the rename.

### 2. Cost arithmetic

For every cell, verify `TotalCost == RAMCost + MissPenaltyAgg` within
`rel_tol = 1e-9`. Field-level integrity check after schema sanity.

### 3. Data-source guardrail

Reads `data_source` from each envelope and groups by `source` field
(`rpc` / `statediff` / `mock`). Mixed sources in the same sweep are
flagged: a Transfer-log surrogate run (`rpc`) and a real read+write
trace (`statediff`) observe different slot universes, so any cost
comparison across them is silently wrong. The full capability dict for
each source (one per family) is kept in the summary so a reader can
confirm the extractor stamps match what they expected.

### 4. Grid completeness

Detects missing or duplicated `(policy, â„“)` cells. The "expected" grid
is the cartesian product of policies and â„“ values *actually observed*
in the input â€” we don't know the *intended* sweep; this is the closest
honest proxy. Duplicates usually mean a half-cleaned re-run left two
files for the same cell.

### 5. Degenerate-cell flag

A cell is flagged only when **all three** of these are true (so a single
extreme metric isn't enough to trip the flag):

| Metric | Default threshold | Meaning when tripped |
|---|---|---|
| `RAMRatio` | `< 0.001` | Policy keeps < 0.1% of slots hot â€” "demote almost everything" |
| `SlotBlocksPruned / TotalExposure` | `> 0.99` | > 99% of slot-block exposure was pruned |
| `HotHitCoverage` | `< 0.30` | < 30% of accesses landed on a hot slot |

Tunable via `--ramratio-degen`, `--pruned-frac-degen`, `--hot-hit-degen`.
Defaults are chosen so the "policy minimises `TotalCost` by demoting
everything and just paying the miss penalty" regime is caught, but a
genuinely sparse hot set (small but not pathological) is not.

### 6. Monotonic trends (`statistical`, `statistical-robust` only)

For threshold-based policies, the keep-hot rule is `p_i(Ï„) > p* = cÂ·Ï„/â„“`
(equivalently, demote when `p_i(Ï„) < p*`),
so as â„“ grows, `p*` falls and the policy keeps more slots hot. Two
properties should hold along the â„“ axis:

- `RAMRatio` non-decreasing in â„“
- `Reactivations` non-increasing in â„“

Violations almost always mean the threshold logic regressed or the
extractor's slot universe shifted between cells. Not run for
`fixed-*` (idle threshold is in blocks, not â„“-driven) or `no-prune`
(`RAMRatio â‰¡ 1`, `Reactivations â‰¡ 0`).

### 7. Same-RAM matches (auto-paired)

Cost comparisons across cells with different `RAMRatio` are
apples-to-oranges â€” a smaller hot set always has lower `RAMCost` just
by definition. For every â„“, the script emits one match per
`(policy_a, policy_b)` pair whose `RAMRatio` differs by less than
`eps` (default `0.005` = 0.5pp). Replaces the earlier hand-tuned band
because the band would silently miss comparable pairs that happened
to fall just outside it. Tunable via `--same-ram-eps`.

## Current results (sweep_v2 â€” 4 policies Ã— 10 â„“ values, 40 cells)

| Check | Result |
|---|---|
| Schema sanity | **0 / 40** issues |
| Cost arithmetic | **0 / 40** failures |
| Data-source guardrail | **OK** â€” single source: `rpc` |
| Grid completeness | **40 / 40** cells, 0 missing, 0 duplicate |
| Degenerate-cell flag | **1** â€” `statistical@â„“=32` (all three reasons) |
| Monotonic trends | **0** violations across `statistical`, `statistical-robust` |
| Same-RAM matches (`\|Î”\| < 0.005`) | **1** pair |

The single same-RAM pair is the same one called out in the previous
narrative â€” at â„“=512, `fixed-1k` and `statistical` both land at
RAMRatio â‰ˆ 6.13 %:

| | fixed-1k | statistical | Î” (fixed-1k âˆ’ stat) |
|---|---:|---:|---:|
| RAMRatio | 0.06156 | 0.06130 | +0.00026 |
| HotHitCoverage | 0.9209 | 0.8157 | **+0.1051** |
| FalsePruneRate | 0.0791 | 0.1843 | **âˆ’0.1051** |
| TotalCost | 32,884,229 | 40,549,660 | **âˆ’7,665,431** |

At the same RAM budget, `fixed-1k` beats `statistical` on coverage by
~10.5 pp at this single comparable cell. This is one â„“ at one RAM band,
not a sweep-wide claim â€” see
[.local/scroll_100k_notes.md](../../../../.local/scroll_100k_notes.md)
for the full caveat list.

The `statistical@â„“=32` row is degenerate (RAMRatio 0.04 %, pruned_frac
99.96 %, HotHitCoverage 18.8 %) â€” at this cost ratio the threshold drops
low enough that the policy demotes almost every slot and just pays the
miss penalty. Don't cite â„“=32 numbers as a model result.

## When to refresh

Re-run after any of:
- A new cost-sweep over different â„“ values lands in `sweep_*/`.
- Field names in `simulate_*.json` change (the schema-sanity check
  catches the rename, but only after you re-run).
- The QA thresholds are tuned (commit the new `qa_summary.json` so
  the next reader sees the new defaults).
- A new extractor source (e.g. `statediff`) lands in the run dir.
